items.briefing:
A new report has found that major ASX 200 companies have been compromised by infostealer malware, which silently harvests login credentials and sensitive data from infected devices. This is a reminder that even large, well-resourced organisations are vulnerable. Separately, a security warning has been issued about SonicWall network devices — even patched versions may still have a weakness that allows attackers to bypass multi-factor authentication, so IT teams should check their configurations urgently.

Australian SMEs are also being reminded not to treat cybersecurity as optional, as threats continue to grow across all sectors. **Practical tip:** If your organisation uses SonicWall devices, contact your IT provider today to confirm your settings are secure, even if recent patches have been applied.

featured.title: ASX 200 firms hit by infostealer infections: report
featured.quote: Australia's largest listed companies exposed to credential theft and supplier compromise risks.
